title: Feature Adoption Leaderboard

landmines.name: count-star-inflation
landmines.kills: naive/1_countstar.sql -- COUNT(*) (or COUNT(user_id)) counts repeat usage by the same person plus double-fired duplicate events, so it both inflates the numbers and reorders the leaderboard; the metric is COUNT(DISTINCT user_id).

notes: Adoption is measured per feature over one calendar month (2024-02) using a half-open range on event_ts ('2024-02-01' <= event_ts < '2024-03-01') so it is portable across engines (string/timestamp comparison, no per-engine date function). events.flag_id is NULL on every non-feature_used row, so the event_type='feature_used' filter plus the INNER JOIN on flag_id both keep those rows out. flag_key is unique and never NULL, so the DESC-adopters / ASC-key ordering is deterministic across engines. Anti-hardcode: the hidden fixture is a different seed at blue scale, so the sample month's numbers do not transfer.
